# SpsFW

> PHP 8.2+ framework for REST APIs with attribute-based routing, built-in DI container, DTO validation, JWT authentication, Redis, and RabbitMQ queue support.

SpsFW follows an attribute-oriented architecture: routes, validation, access control, and dependency injection are declared directly on controller methods and constructor parameters using PHP 8 attributes. There are no config files for routing — everything is co-located with the code.

## Getting Started

- [Installation & Project Structure](docs/1_getting_started.md)
- [Configuration (.env, Config::init)](docs/7_configuration.md)
- [Database Migrations (Phinx)](docs/8_database_migrations.md)

- [Routing (#[Route], #[Controller])](docs/2_routing.md)
- [Controllers (RestController, response helpers)](docs/3_controllers.md)
- [Validation (DTO, #[JsonBody], #[QueryParams])](docs/4_validation.md)
- [Dependency Injection (#[Inject], DIContainer)](docs/6_dependency_injection.md)
- [Middleware (#[Middleware], MiddlewareInterface)](docs/5_middleware.md)
- [Access Control (#[AccessRulesAll], #[AccessRulesAny])](docs/9_access_control.md)
- [Exceptions (BaseException, HTTP codes)](docs/10_exceptions.md)

- [Queue Workers (RabbitMQ)](docs/11_queue_workes.md)
